The COVID-19 pandemic had a significant impact on Zeekr, particularly in the early stages of the outbreak. In early 2022, vehicle production at the Zeekr Factory was temporarily suspended due to pandemic-related restrictions. Additionally, retail stores and delivery centers in key cities like Shanghai, Shenzhen, and Xi’an were temporarily closed, which adversely affected vehicle deliveries, marketing efforts, and the expansion of retail networks. For instance, vehicle deliveries in January, February, and March 2022 were 3,530 units, 2,916 units, and 1,795 units, respectively, reflecting the disruptions caused by the pandemic.

The pandemic also impacted Zeekr’s research and development (R&D) efficiency, as travel restrictions hindered face-to-face interactions and testing with Sweden-based R&D talents at CEVT. Furthermore, the supply of certain raw materials and auto parts experienced fluctuations due to COVID-19, although Zeekr’s advanced planning and effective supply chain management helped mitigate significant disruptions or cost increases.

Despite these challenges, the BEV industry in China began to recover in 2022, supported by favorable government policies aimed at incentivizing NEV purchases. For example, the extension of the vehicle purchase tax exemption for NEVs until the end of 2023 helped stimulate market demand. By the end of 2022, the BEV industry in China had achieved notable growth, with sales reaching 5.4 million units, an 81.6% increase compared to 2021.

Zeekr has continued to monitor and evaluate the impact of COVID-19 and other potential disease outbreaks, taking appropriate measures to reduce their impact on business operations. The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited may not succeed in continuing to maintain and strengthen the Zeekr brand, and its brand and reputation could be harmed by negative publicity and customer complaints regarding ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, its products, or its services. Any dysfunction or outdated developments in Southeast Asia may negatively affect the production of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's battery electric vehicles (BEVs). Any problems or delays in ramping up and maintaining operations of the Vehicle Manufacturing Factories could negatively affect the production of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's BEVs. failure to develop appropriate risk management and internal control structures tailored to overseas operations; difficulty and cost relating to compliance with different commercial and legal requirements of the overseas markets in which ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited offers or plans to offer its products and services including charging and other electric infrastructures; failure to obtain or maintain permits for ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's products or services in these markets; different safety concerns and measures needed to address accident-related risks in different countries and regions; inability to obtain, maintain or enforce intellectual property rights; unanticipated changes in prevailing economic conditions and regulatory requirements; ineligibility for tax credits or other incentives offered by governments in these markets; and   
adverse impact from economic sanctions and trade barriers such as export, import and international trade controls, tariffs, taxes and other restrictions and expenses.

Lotus Technology has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group for the manufacturing of its Zeekr vehicles at the Zeekr Factory, the Chengdu Factory, the Meishan Factory, and the Chunxiao Factory (together, “Vehicle Manufacturing Factories”). Lotus Technology's future operation and prospects depend on the successful ramping and maintaining of operations in these factories. See “Item 4. Information on the Company—4.B. Business Overview— Manufacturing, Supply Chain and Quality Control.” There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology's oversight on battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing will always be effective, as the Vehicle Manufacturing Factories are not owned by Lotus Technology. Vehicle production at such factories may also experience delays or suspensions. For example, vehicle production at the Zeekr Factory was temporarily suspended in early 2022 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and Lotus Technology also encountered a temporary suspension in production at the Zeekr Factory in the first quarter of 2023 due to production line upgrades. As of the date of this annual report, Lotus Technology has not experienced any material customer complaints caused by the delays or suspensions of vehicle production at the Vehicle Manufacturing Factories. In addition, there will be a negative impact on Lotus Technology's business operation if, in the future, such original equipment manufacturer (OEM) model is limited by applicable laws and regulations, or becomes subject to more stringent regulatory oversight. Given the size and complexity of the manufacturing of BEVs, it is possible that the Vehicle Manufacturing Factories may experience issues or delays in further expanding their production output.

The COVID-19 pandemic has negatively impacted Lotus Technology's business operations and financial performance. In particular, Lotus Technology has experienced occasional delays, interruptions, suspensions, and temporary closures in production, delivery, sales and marketing, research and development efforts, and the supply chain due to travel, workplace, or social restrictions. • Vehicle production in ZEEKR Factory was temporarily suspended in early 2022. • In the beginning of 2022, Lotus Technology temporarily closed the retail stores and delivery centers in Shanghai, Shenzhen, and Xi’an. The company's vehicle delivery, marketing, and the expansion of retail stores had been adversely affected. In January, February, and March 2022, Lotus Technology's vehicle deliveries were 3,530 units, 2,916 units, and 1,795 units, respectively. • Due to travel difficulties worldwide, Lotus Technology had to suspend the usual face-to-face interaction and testing with Sweden-based R&D talents in CEVT, which adversely affected the company's R&D efficiency. • In early 2022, the supply of certain raw materials for Lotus Technology's production and the delivery of certain auto parts experienced fluctuation due to COVID-19. Due to Lotus Technology's advanced planning and effective supply chain management, Lotus Technology has not experienced significant disruptions to its supply chain or significant increases in costs as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic. However, uncertainties remain as to whether and to what extent the market demand and the battery electric vehicle (BEV) supply chain may be affected by COVID-19 and other disease outbreaks and pandemics in the future.

In light of the uncertainties in the global market and economic conditions that can be created by future disease outbreaks and pandemics, Lotus Technology will continue to monitor and evaluate such matters and take appropriate measures to reduce their impact on its business operations. See also “Risk Factors — Risks Related to Lotus Technology's Business and Industry — The COVID-19 outbreak has adversely affected, and may continue to adversely affect, Lotus Technology's results of operations.

There have been some new waves of COVID-19 in certain cities in 2022 while the related impacts such as restricted logistics and tight upstream supply were relatively short-term and limited for the battery electric vehicle (BEV) industry in China. Meanwhile, a series of favorable policies were issued to incentivize consumers to purchase BEVs. In May 2022, the “Opinions on Fiscal Support to Achieve Carbon Peaking and Carbon Neutrality Goals” published by the Ministry of Finance (MoF) urged implementing preferential tax policies and improving government procurement policies for new energy vehicles (NEVs). In August 2022, according to a State Council meeting, the vehicle purchase tax exemption for NEVs will be extended to the end of 2023, aiming to encourage the sustainable development of the NEV market under the impact of COVID-19. Therefore, with the continuous support of the governments and resumption of production and logistics, the BEV industry has recovered gradually with production and sales of BEVs achieving notable growth in 2022. According to data released by the China Association of Automobile Manufacturers, in 2022, the sales volume of BEVs in China was 5.4 million units, representing an increase of 81.6% compared with 2021.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Zeekr 001, Zeekr's first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV), is a premium crossover shooting brake developed with proprietary research and development (R&D), deep market insights, and the Sustainable Experience Architecture (SEA) platform. Launched in April 2021 with deliveries beginning in October 2021, the Zeekr 001 was the first mass-produced BEV with over $1,000 kilometers CLTC range. In October 2023, Zeekr introduced the Zeekr 001 FR, a high-performance variant featuring a lightweight carbon fiber body, 800V Qilin battery, and quad e-drive system, achieving 2.02 seconds 0-100 kilometers per hour acceleration and a 280 kilometers per hour top speed. In 2024, Zeekr launched upgraded versions of the Zeekr 001 with Zeekr Intelligent Driving 2.0 and significant performance, safety, and intelligence upgrades.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Zeekr’s smart cockpit is powered by Zeekr OS, an exclusive distributed operating system designed for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), seamlessly integrated with Zeekr's state-of-the-art electrical and electronic (E/E) architecture. By dynamically allocating processing power to services, functions, and applications in real-time, Zeekr OS optimizes cockpit interactions, autonomous driving, and vehicle control, ensuring a seamless and highly responsive user experience. Zeekr OS features a unified service-oriented architecture, in-house full-stack research and development (R&D), optimized network diagnostics, and automated software integration testing, eliminating system lag and enabling continuous updates. With multi-kernel compatibility (Linux, QNX), Zeekr OS supports 360-degree surround-view monitoring, parking assistance, and intelligent energy management, setting a new benchmark for BEV software architecture. The smart cockpit experience is further enhanced by the Qualcomm 8295 SoC-based platform, standard across all Zeekr vehicle models launched in 2024, with the Zeekr 009 Grand featuring the world’s first dual-8295 configuration. The Zeekr Sound audio system, equipped with AI-powered tuning, delivers premium acoustic performance, while the Human-Machine Interface (HMI) 3.5 introduces 3D navigation, advanced visual effects, and an intuitive Eva family interface. EVA, the AI-powered driving assistant, leverages machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and computer vision to provide intelligent voice interaction, predictive assistance, and customizable AI personas. Additionally, Zeekr’s open cockpit ecosystem integrates a virtualized mobile platform, a Scenario Library, and Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity, allowing seamless integration with smart home devices, gaming, and entertainment applications such as Kid Zone, Tencent Cloud Gaming, and wireless karaoke.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Smart Cockpit 
summary: Zeekr's smart cockpit, set to launch in 2024, is powered by Zeekr OS, a specialized distributed operating system designed for battery electric vehicles (BEVs). This system integrates advanced electronic and electrical architecture, enabling real-time processing power allocation to enhance user interactions, autonomous driving, and vehicle control. Key features include a unified service-oriented architecture, in-house R&D, improved network diagnostics, and automated software integration testing, which together minimize system lag and support continuous updates. The multi-kernel compatibility allows for advanced functionalities like 360-degree surround-view monitoring and intelligent energy management, setting a new benchmark for BEV software.

The smart cockpit experience is further elevated by the Qualcomm 8295 SoC platform, particularly in the Zeekr 009 Grand, which boasts a dual-8295 configuration. The Zeekr Sound audio system delivers premium sound through AI-driven tuning, while the HMI 3.5 offers advanced 3D navigation and visual effects, enhanced by the intuitive Eva family interface. EVA, the AI assistant, leverages machine learning and natural language processing for intelligent voice interactions and customizable AI personas. The open cockpit ecosystem promotes a virtualized mobile platform and IoT connectivity, allowing integration with smart home devices and entertainment options like Kid Zone, Tencent Cloud Gaming, and wireless karaoke. Overall, Zeekr's smart cockpit, driven by Zeekr OS and AI advancements, aims to provide an immersive and connected driving experience.

title: NOTES TO THE COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2021, 2022 AND 2023 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: Loss per share (LPS) is a key financial metric that reflects the loss attributable to each outstanding share of a company's stock, calculated by dividing the net loss by the total number of shares. This metric is vital for investors as it provides insight into a company's financial performance on a per-share basis, with higher LPS indicating greater losses that can adversely affect investor sentiment and stock valuation. 

Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, has reported its LPS figures from its inception. The company's net loss from consolidated entities increased significantly from approximately $2.3 billion in 2021 to around $7.7 billion in 2022, before slightly rising to about $8.3 billion in 2023. In contrast, its subsidiary Ningbo Viridi transitioned from a net loss of approximately $151 million in 2021 to a net income of $278 million in 2022, maintaining a positive net income of about $82 million in 2023. 

The net loss attributable to ordinary shareholders rose sharply from approximately $2.1 billion in 2021 to nearly $7.9 billion in 2022, and further to about $8.3 billion in 2023. The weighted average number of ordinary shares outstanding increased from approximately 1.5 billion in 2021 to 2 billion in both 2022 and 2023, leading to a basic net loss per share that escalated from $(1.43) in 2021 to $(3.97) in 2022, and $(4.17) in 2023.

The calculations for diluted net loss per share excluded certain restricted share units and convertible preferred shares due to their anti-dilutive effects. The types of shares involved include restricted share units, Series Pre-A preferred shares, and Series A preferred shares, each representing different stages of equity ownership and often used to incentivize employees and attract investors.

A detailed overview of share counts relevant for dilution calculations shows that the number of restricted share units and Series Pre-A preferred shares increased from 2021 to 2023, reflecting the evolving equity structure of Zeekr. This data is crucial for understanding the implications of share dynamics on dilution calculations over the specified fiscal years.

metadata.title_summary:
title: IMPACT OF COVID-19 ON OUR OPERATIONS AND FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has significantly disrupted our business operations and financial performance, causing delays and temporary closures in production, delivery, sales, and supply chain activities due to various restrictions. In early 2022, ZEEKR Factory experienced notable challenges, including halted vehicle production and the closure of retail stores and delivery centers in major cities like Shanghai, Shenzhen, and Xi’an, which severely impacted vehicle deliveries and marketing efforts. Delivery figures dropped sharply in the first quarter, with only 3,530, 2,916, and 1,795 units delivered in January, February, and March, respectively. Additionally, travel restrictions hampered R&D collaboration with personnel in Sweden, further affecting development efficiency and leading to supply chain disruptions that caused fluctuations in the availability of essential raw materials and auto parts. Despite these challenges, the company has managed to minimize disruptions and cost increases through advanced planning and effective supply chain management. However, uncertainties regarding the potential impact of future pandemics on market demand and the BEV supply chain remain, prompting the company to actively monitor global conditions and implement strategies to mitigate adverse effects on operations. Overall, while the pandemic has negatively impacted our business, we remain committed to navigating these challenges and safeguarding our performance.

title: 
summary: The financial overview of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, as detailed in the combined and consolidated statements for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, reveals significant changes in shareholders' equity and overall financial performance. The company began 2021 with no issued shares and total equity of RMB 3,379,969. Throughout the years, it underwent substantial transactions, including a capital injection of RMB 500,000 and the issuance of 2 billion ordinary shares, which contributed to a total paid-in capital of RMB 4,269,555 by the end of 2021. However, the company faced challenges, including a cumulative comprehensive loss of RMB 4,584,927, resulting in a total equity of RMB 929,426.

By 2023, Zeekr reported 2 billion ordinary shares and approximately 265.8 million preferred shares, with additional paid-in capital rising to RMB 11.2 billion. Despite these increases, the accumulated deficit escalated to RMB (20.9 billion), reflecting a net loss of RMB (8.3 billion) for the year. The total shareholders' equity decreased to RMB (8.7 billion), indicating ongoing financial difficulties.

The cash flow statements highlighted the company's struggles, with net losses from operating activities growing from RMB 4.5 billion in 2021 to RMB 8.3 billion in 2023. Share-based compensation and depreciation expenses also rose, underscoring the financial strain. The adjustments in accounts receivable and inventory further illustrated the challenges faced by Zeekr in managing its financial health.

Overall, the combined financial statements provide a comprehensive view of Zeekr's equity structure, financial performance, and cash flow dynamics, emphasizing the impact of various transactions and losses on the company's fiscal standing.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, as detailed in the combined and consolidated statements for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2021, 2022, and 2023, reveals significant changes in shareholders' equity and overall financial performance. The company began 2021 with no issued shares and total equity of RMB 3,379,969. Throughout the years, it underwent substantial transactions, including a capital injection of RMB 500,000 and the issuance of 2 billion ordinary shares, which contributed to a total paid-in capital of RMB 4,269,555 by the end of 2021. However, the company faced challenges, including a cumulative comprehensive loss of RMB 4,584,927, resulting in a total equity of RMB 929,426.

By 2023, Zeekr reported 2 billion ordinary shares and approximately 265.8 million preferred shares, with additional paid-in capital rising to RMB 11.2 billion. Despite these increases, the accumulated deficit escalated to RMB (20.9 billion), reflecting a net loss of RMB (8.3 billion) for the year. The total shareholders' equity decreased to RMB (8.7 billion), indicating ongoing financial difficulties.

The cash flow statements highlighted the company's struggles, with net losses from operating activities growing from RMB 4.5 billion in 2021 to RMB 8.3 billion in 2023. Share-based compensation and depreciation expenses also rose, underscoring the financial strain. The adjustments in accounts receivable and inventory further illustrated the challenges faced by Zeekr in managing its financial health.

Overall, the combined financial statements provide a comprehensive view of Zeekr's equity structure, financial performance, and cash flow dynamics, emphasizing the impact of various transactions and losses on the company's fiscal standing.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: There have been some new waves of COVID-19 in certain cities in 2022 while the related impacts such as restricted logistics and tight upstream supply were relatively short-term and limited for the battery electric vehicle (BEV) industry in China. Meanwhile, a series of favorable policies were issued to incentivize consumers to purchase BEVs. In May 2022, the “Opinions on Fiscal Support to Achieve Carbon Peaking and Carbon Neutrality Goals” published by the Ministry of Finance (MoF) urged implementing preferential tax policies and improving government procurement policies for new energy vehicles (NEVs). In August 2022, according to a State Council meeting, the vehicle purchase tax exemption for NEVs will be extended to the end of 2023, aiming to encourage the sustainable development of the NEV market under the impact of COVID-19. Therefore, with the continuous support of the governments and resumption of production and logistics, the BEV industry has recovered gradually with production and sales of BEVs achieving notable growth in 2022. According to data released by the China Association of Automobile Manufacturers, in 2022, the sales volume of BEVs in China was 5.4 million units, representing an increase of 81.6% compared with 2021.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Impact of the COVID-19 Pandemic on the BEV Industry in China 
summary: In 2022, despite facing challenges from new COVID-19 waves, China's Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) industry experienced a limited and short-term impact on logistics and supply. To stimulate the sector, the Chinese government introduced various supportive measures, including favorable tax policies and enhanced government procurement for new energy vehicles (NEVs), as outlined in the Ministry of Finance's "Opinions on Fiscal Support to Achieve Carbon Peaking and Carbon Neutrality Goals." Furthermore, a State Council meeting extended the vehicle purchase tax exemption for NEVs through the end of 2023 to encourage market growth during the pandemic. These initiatives, coupled with the resumption of production and logistics, facilitated a gradual recovery in the BEV market, resulting in sales of 5.4 million units in 2022—an impressive 81.6% increase from the previous year, according to the China Association of Automobile Manufacturers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has entered into Cooperation Framework Agreements with Geely Group for the manufacturing of the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, and ZEEKR 009 at the ZEEKR Factory, the manufacturing of the ZEEKR X at the Chengdu Factory, and the manufacturing of the upscale sedan model at the Meishan Factory. Lotus Technology's future operation and prospects depend on the successful ramping and maintaining of operations in the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory. See “Business — Manufacturing and Quality Control.” There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology's oversight on battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing will always be effective, as the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory are not owned by Lotus Technology. Vehicle production at such factories may also experience delays or suspensions. For example, vehicle production at the ZEEKR Factory was temporarily suspended in early 2022 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, and Lotus Technology also encountered a temporary suspension in production at the ZEEKR Factory in the first quarter of 2023 due to production line upgrades at the factory. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not experienced any material customer complaints caused by the delays or suspensions of vehicle production at the ZEEKR Factory, the Chengdu Factory, and the Meishan Factory. In addition, there will be a negative impact on Lotus Technology's business operation if, in the future, such original equipment manufacturer (OEM) model is limited by applicable laws and regulations, or becomes subject to more stringent regulatory oversight.

The success of the company's future operations relies heavily on its ability to effectively ramp up and maintain production at these sites. However, oversight of Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) manufacturing may be compromised since the facilities are not owned by the company, leading to potential delays or suspensions, as evidenced by the temporary halts at the Zeekr Factory in early 2022 and early 2023. Despite these interruptions, customer complaints have remained minimal. Nonetheless, future challenges could arise from regulatory changes affecting the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) model, as well as the inherent complexities of BEV manufacturing, which may pose additional risks to the company's operations, prospects, and financial health. For more detailed information, refer to the section on Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control in the company's business overview.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The success of Lotus Technology's business ultimately depends on consumer spending. Lotus Technology derives a substantial part of its revenues from China. As a result, Lotus Technology's revenues and financial results are impacted to a significant extent by economic conditions in China and globally. The global macroeconomic environment is facing challenges, including the economic slowdown in the Eurozone since 2014, the potential impact of the United Kingdom’s exit from the EU on January 31, 2020, and the adverse impact on global economies and financial markets from the COVID-19 pandemic. There is considerable uncertainty over the long-term effects of the expansionary monetary and fiscal policies adopted by the central banks and financial authorities of some of the world’s leading economies, including the United States and China. There have been concerns over unrest and terrorist threats in the Middle East, Europe, and Africa, as well as over the conflicts involving Ukraine, Syria, and North Korea. There have also been concerns about the relationship between China and other Asian countries, which may result in or intensify potential conflicts related to territorial disputes and the trade disputes between the United States and China. The ongoing trade tensions between the United States and China may have a tremendous negative impact on the economies of not merely the two countries concerned, but the global economy as a whole. It is unclear whether these challenges and uncertainties will be contained or resolved, and what effects they may have on global political and economic conditions in the long term.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A severe or prolonged downturn in the PRC or global economy could materially and adversely affect our business, results of operations and financial condition. 
summary: The success of our business is closely tied to consumer spending, especially in China, which plays a crucial role in our revenue and financial health. Currently, we are navigating a challenging global macroeconomic landscape marked by a prolonged economic slowdown in the Eurozone, uncertainties related to the UK's EU exit, and the lingering effects of the COVID-19 pandemic on economies worldwide. Furthermore, the long-term implications of expansive monetary and fiscal policies in major economies, including the U.S. and China, remain uncertain. Geopolitical tensions, particularly trade disputes between the U.S. and China, add to the complexity, posing risks not only to the involved nations but also to the global economy. 

China's economic performance is significantly shaped by global trends and domestic policies, and while it has experienced substantial growth over the years, this growth has been uneven and has recently slowed. Concerns about potential downturns could lead to tighter credit conditions, increased market volatility, and diminished consumer and business confidence, all of which could hinder our ability to raise capital effectively. Sales of high-end and luxury products, such as performance electric vehicles, are particularly susceptible to changes in discretionary spending, and economic uncertainty may prompt consumers to delay or reduce their purchases, potentially harming our operational performance.

title: A severe or prolonged downturn in the PRC or global economy could materially and adversely affect our business, results of operations and financial condition. 
summary: The success of our business is closely tied to consumer spending, particularly in China, making our financial performance sensitive to both Chinese and global economic conditions. Currently, the global economy faces several challenges, including a prolonged slowdown in the Eurozone, uncertainties from Brexit, and the economic fallout from the COVID-19 pandemic. The long-term effects of expansive monetary and fiscal policies by major economies, such as the U.S. and China, remain uncertain, compounded by geopolitical tensions and conflicts in regions like the Middle East, Ukraine, Syria, and North Korea. Additionally, the relationship between China and other Asian nations introduces risks of territorial disputes and trade tensions with the U.S., which could destabilize both regional and global economies.

China's economic landscape is shaped by global trends and domestic policies, exhibiting uneven growth across different sectors and regions, with recent signs of slowing. While the economy remains relatively stable, there are risks of fluctuations that could lead to tighter credit markets and decreased consumer confidence. Such economic uncertainty can significantly impact discretionary spending on high-end and luxury products, including performance electric vehicles. If consumers perceive economic instability, they may reduce or delay purchases, which could adversely affect our operational results and overall business performance.
